verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is canonically a secreted serine protease with a cleavable N-terminal signal peptide and no membrane anchor. However, robust recent evidence demonstrates cell surface expression in prostate cancer cells, with clinical validation via multiple cell-surface-directed therapeutics (bispecific T-cell engager pasritamig, CAR-T, radioligand). This surface display is cancer-cell-state-induced: the protein body reaches the outer leaflet as a documented hallmark of oncogenic transformation in prostate cancer, distinct from its baseline secreted form. The mechanism of cancer-cell-surface display (whether via secretory trafficking variants, protein-protein tethering, or other routes) is not yet fully characterized, but the clinical and preclinical efficacy data unambiguously establish surface accessibility in the relevant disease context.
